message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for INDEX 2 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the finding at index 2 in the JSON array.

content.content: 1	[{"issue": "102", "title": "run_json applies retries multiplicatively, causing a retry-storm and unbounded blocking time with no overall deadline", "file": "src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py", "sev": "HIGH", "problem": "`run_json` loops `MAX_RETRIES+1` times (line 531) and each iteration calls `_run_with_retries` (line 542), which itself retries up to `MAX_RETRIES` transient attempts AND up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` rate-limit attempts. The budgets multiply. PoC (transient errors every attempt): `_attempt` is invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. Worse, the rate-limit path: per outer iteration the rate budget sleeps sum(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4) = 450s; times 3 outer iteratio", "fix_update": "run_json now re-rolls ONLY on JSON parse failure (transient/rate/permanent propagate, no budget multiplication) + _sleep_bounded bounds inner backoff by the overall deadline. New tests: no-multiplication, seed re-roll on parse failure, deadline bound."}, {"issue": "111", "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py", "sev": "MEDIUM", "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions ", "fix_update": "make_gate_node already accepts recorder= (bridges to ProvenanceTrail); now GOVERNANCE \u00a73 + graph.py docstring honestly scope that the durable graph path needs the recorder to be hash-chained. Test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail."}, {"issue": "113", "title": "GateReview is a frozen dataclass whose Mapping payload is not frozen \u2014 mutable-in-place and unhashable (issue #7 escape)", "file": "src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "GateReview is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with a `payload: Mapping[str, Any]` field but, unlike EVERY other frozen mapping-bearing value record in the engine (ProvenanceEntry, GateDecision, Corpus, ClaimRecord, Verdict, Hypothesis, BaselineArtifact, ReasoningRequest, etc.), it has no __post_init__ that normalizes the field through freeze_mapping. This reproduces both halves of the codebase's own documented issue #7 (see src/assay_engine/_frozen.py module docstring):\n\n1. Mutable-", "fix_update": "GateReview.__post_init__ freezes payload; NEW test test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable asserts FrozenDict + mutation raises + hashable."}, {"issue": "114", "title": "ProvenanceTrail.record performs a non-atomic check-then-act on shared list state (not thread-safe)", "file": "src/assay_engine/provenance.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "record() computes `seq = len(self._entries)` and `prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash` (read), then does work (clock, digest, freeze), then `self._entries.append(entry)` (write), with no lock. If a single ProvenanceTrail were shared across threads, two concurrent record() calls could read the same seq/prev_hash and append two entries that both claim e.g. seq=N with the same prev_hash, breaking the chain so verify_records() (which asserts e.seq == i and e.prev_hash == prev) would fail \u2014 a co", "fix_update": "record() lock-guarded; NEW test test_record_is_thread_safe_under_concurrency (8 threads x50, verify + contiguous seqs)."}, {"issue": "117", "title": "QdrantVectorStore owns and never closes its self-constructed client (connection/handle leak)", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "When no client is injected, `QdrantVectorStore.__init__` constructs its own `QdrantClient` via `get_qdrant_client()` (line 64) and stores it on `self._client`, but the class exposes no `close()`/context-manager and never closes the client. `QdrantClient` holds an underlying HTTP connection pool (httpx) / gRPC channel; for a self-constructed client the store is the sole owner, so the connection resources are leaked for the lifetime of every store instance with no deterministic release. Long-runni", "fix_update": "_owns_client + close()/context-manager; NEW test test_vector_store_closes_owned_client (owned client closed via get_qdrant_client + context exit)."}, {"issue": "118", "title": "QdrantVectorStore.upsert materializes all PointStructs (with per-vector copies) before sending any batch, so batching bounds payload but not memory", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "upsert builds the complete `points` list \u2014 one PointStruct per id, each holding `list(v)` (a fresh copy of the whole vector) \u2014 for the entire input before the chunking loop at line 88 sends anything. The comment claims chunking handles 'a large ingest', but it only bounds each HTTP request payload; peak process memory still holds the full corpus of vectors duplicated into PointStructs at once. For a real ingest (e.g. 5M docs x 768 float dims) this is the entire embedding set re-copied into Pytho", "fix_update": "upsert builds one batch at a time; NEW test test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time asserts only batch_size vectors consumed per send (2,4,6 not 6,6,6)."}, {"issue": "119", "title": "corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly per study (ingest + every build_baseline_artifact call), each a full canonical copy + sort + JSON of the whole corpus", "file": "src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "corpus_fingerprint builds a full list-comprehension copy of every unit and relation, sorts all units by id, canonicalizes every attribute mapping via _plain, and json.dumps the whole thing \u2014 O(total corpus bytes) time and memory. run_study computes it once at ingest (pipeline.py:280, `cfp`). Then build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:104) computes it again from scratch for the determinism record's `input_hashes['corpus']` and `corpus_fingerprint`; a study's BaselineBuilder that calls build_bas", "fix_update": "build_baseline_artifact accepts corpus_hash (no recompute); NEW test test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash. NOTE: full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is deliberately deferred (would require a breaking build() signature change for a LOW perf item) \u2014 documented scope."}, {"issue": "120", "title": "Package version is hardcoded in two independent places (pyproject `version` and `__init__.__version__`), and the second feeds the reproducibility/provenance manifest", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`pyproject.toml` sets `version = \"0.0.1\"` (line 7) while `src/assay_engine/__init__.py` separately hardcodes `__version__ = \"0.0.1\"` (line 28). These are two independent strings with no single source of truth \u2014 hatchling is not configured for dynamic versioning (`grep dynamic`/`hatch.version` returns nothing). This is not merely a style nit: `src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py` imports `__version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION` (line 24) and stamps it into the determinism/provenance record as `compo", "fix_update": "version dynamic + hatch.version path; NEW tests in test_packaging.py (no static version, dynamic declared, dist==__version__)."}, {"issue": "121", "title": "mypy is pinned to `python_version = \"3.11\"` but CI advertises and matrix-tests up to 3.14, so 3.12\u20133.14-only type errors are never caught", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`[tool.mypy] python_version = \"3.11\"` forces mypy to type-check the code as if running on Python 3.11 regardless of the runner's interpreter. The core CI lane runs mypy across a 3.11\u20133.14 matrix and the integration lane runs it on 3.12, but because the analysis target is fixed at 3.11, type behavior that differs across versions (e.g. stdlib stubs that gained/changed signatures in 3.12+, version-gated typing constructs) is only ever validated at the 3.11 floor. The project classifies and matrix-t", "fix_update": "mypy python_version removed; NEW test test_mypy_is_not_pinned_to_a_single_python_version parses pyproject."}, {"issue": "122", "title": "run_study emits phase/reasoning spans but never starts tracing or stamps run_id baggage \u2014 traces cannot be correlated to the run or the experiment-tracker run_id", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "run_study is the documented end-to-end composed runner and it opens trace spans for every phase (tracer.span('phase:INGEST') etc.) and the reasoning seam emits LLM/AGENT spans underneath. But run_study never calls bootstrap_tracing() and never wraps execution in run_trace_context(run_id) \u2014 grep of pipeline.py shows no reference to either. run_trace_context (tracing.py:145-163) is what stamps the 'assay.run_id' baggage onto every span so traces are correlatable across stores; the orchestration gr", "fix_update": "run_study calls bootstrap_tracing + wraps phases in run_trace_context(corr_id); NEW test test_run_study_correlates_spans_to_run_id asserts every phase span sees assay.run_id baggage + detached after."}]

content.text: Index 2 is issue #113 (GateReview frozen dataclass payload not frozen). Let me examine the current state.

content.content:
1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import uuid
33	from contextlib import ExitStack
34	from dataclasses import dataclass
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping
37	
38	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
39	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
40	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
41	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
42	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
43	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
44	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
45	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
46	    ClaimConfirmer,
47	    SourceScorecard,
48	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
49	)
50	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
51	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
52	    ClaimBlindGuard,
53	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
54	    FirewallViolation,
55	)
56	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
57	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
58	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
59	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
60	    OtelTracer,
61	    Tracer,
62	    bootstrap_tracing,
63	    run_trace_context,
64	)
65	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
66	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
67	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
68	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
69	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
70	
71	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
72	
73	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
74	
75	
76	class IngestionError(ValueError):
77	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
78	
79	
80	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
81	class GateReview:
82	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition."""
83	
84	    gate: str
85	    frm: Phase
86	    to: Phase
87	    summary: str
88	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
89	
90	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
91	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
92	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
93	
94	
95	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
96	
97	
98	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
99	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
100	
101	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
102	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
103	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
104	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
105	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
106	    """
107	    return GateDecision(
108	        approved=True,
109	        gate=review.gate,
110	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
111	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
112	    )
113	
114	
115	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
116	class StudyPlan:
117	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
118	
119	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
120	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
121	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
122	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
123	    definition).
124	    """
125	
126	    definition: StudyDefinition
127	    source: Path
128	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
129	    authority: TimestampAuthority
130	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
131	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
132	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
133	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
134	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
135	
136	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
137	        modes = self.definition.modes
138	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
139	            missing = [
140	                n
141	                for n, v in (
142	                    ("split", self.split),
143	                    ("discover", self.discover),
144	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
145	                )
146	                if v is None
147	            ]
148	            if missing:
149	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
150	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
151	            missing = [
152	                n
153	                for n, v in (
154	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
155	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
156	                )
157	                if v is None
158	            ]
159	            if missing:
160	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
161	
162	
163	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
164	class StudyResult:
165	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
166	
167	    study: str
168	    corpus_fingerprint: str
169	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
170	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
171	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
172	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
173	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
174	    source_version: str | None = None
175	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
176	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
177	
178	
179	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
180	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
181	
182	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
183	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
184	
185	    return _log
186	
187	
188	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
189	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
190	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
191	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
192	
193	
194	def run_study(
195	    plan: StudyPlan,
196	    *,
197	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
198	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
199	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
200	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
201	    clock: Clock | None = None,
202	    secret: bytes | None = None,
203	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
204	) -> StudyResult:
205	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
206	
207	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
208	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
209	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
210	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
211	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
212	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
213	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
214	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
215	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
216	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
217	
218	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
219	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
220	    """
221	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
222	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
223	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
224	    defn = plan.definition
225	    modes = defn.modes
226	    required = required_phases(modes)
227	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
228	    visited: list[Phase] = []
229	    run_id: str | None = None
230	
231	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
232	        visited.append(phase)
233	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
234	
235	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
236	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
237	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
238	            return
239	        try:
240	            fn(tracker, run_id)
241	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
242	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
243	
244	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
245	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
246	        decision = gate_handler(review)
247	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
248	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
249	        trail.record(
250	            "gate",
251	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
252	            gate=review.gate,
253	            approved=approved,
254	            reason=reason,
255	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
256	        )
257	        if not approved:
258	            raise GateError(
259	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
260	            )
261	
262	    trail.record(
263	        "run_start",
264	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
265	        study=defn.name,
266	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
267	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
268	    )
269	    if tracker is not None:
270	        try:
271	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
272	                defn.name,
273	                {
274	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
275	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
276	                },
277	            )
278	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
279	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
280	
281	    # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
282	    # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122). Best-effort.
283	    bootstrap_tracing()
284	    corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
285	    ok = False
286	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
287	    trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
288	    try:
289	        # ---- INGEST ----
290	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
291	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
292	            try:
293	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
294	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
295	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
296	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
297	            if not corpus.units:
298	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
299	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
300	            source_version: str | None = None
301	            if versioner is not None:
302	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
303	            trail.record(
304	                "ingest",
305	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
306	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
307	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
308	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
309	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
310	                source_version=source_version,
311	            )
312	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
313	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
314	                assert plan.split is not None
315	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
316	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
317	                if unknown:
318	                    raise FirewallViolation(
319	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
320	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
321	                    )
322	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
323	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
324	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
325	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
326	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
327	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
328	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
329	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
330	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
331	                    raise FirewallViolation(
332	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
333	                    )
334	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
335	                if ghost:
336	                    raise FirewallViolation(
337	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
338	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
339	                    )
340	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
341	                trail.record(
342	                    "features",
343	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
344	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
345	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
346	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
347	                )
348	
349	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
350	        baseline_instant = _now()
351	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
352	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
353	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
354	            with guard.sealed():
355	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
356	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
357	                raise FirewallViolation(
358	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
359	                    "was not built from this corpus"
360	                )
361	            trail.record(
362	                "baseline",
363	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
364	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
365	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
366	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
367	            )
368	
369	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
370	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
371	
372	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
373	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
374	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
375	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
376	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
377	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
378	
379	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
380	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
381	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
382	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
383	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
384	                trail.record(
385	                    "discovery",
386	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
387	                    n=len(hypotheses),
388	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
389	                )
390	
391	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
392	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
393	                if not held_out.units:
394	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
395	                preregister_instant = _now()
396	                for h in hypotheses:
397	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
398	                        raise FirewallViolation(
399	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
400	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
401	                        )
402	                    vt = require_preregistered(
403	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
404	                    )
405	                    trail.record(
406	                        "preregister",
407	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
408	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
409	                        digest=vt.digest,
410	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
411	                    )
412	                run_gate(
413	                    GateReview(
414	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
415	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
416	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
417	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
418	                        payload={
419	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
420	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
421	                        },
422	                    )
423	                )
424	
425	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
426	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
427	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
428	                for h in hypotheses:
429	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
430	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
431	                        raise FirewallViolation(
432	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
433	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
434	                        )
435	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
436	                    trail.record(
437	                        "verdict",
438	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
439	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
440	                        label=verdict.label.value,
441	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
442	                    )
443	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
444	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
445	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
446	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
447	
448	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
449	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
450	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
451	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
452	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
453	            if not claim_records:
454	                raise ValueError(
455	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
456	                )
457	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
458	            run_gate(
459	                GateReview(
460	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
461	                    frm=prev_phase,
462	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
463	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
464	                    payload={
465	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
466	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
467	                        "claim_fingerprint": defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint(),
468	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
469	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
470	                    },
471	                )
472	            )
473	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
474	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
475	
476	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
477	                    trail.record(
478	                        "preregister",
479	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
480	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
481	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
482	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
483	                    )
484	                    trail.record(
485	                        "verdict",
486	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
487	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
488	                        label=v.label.value,
489	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
490	                    )
491	
492	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
493	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
494	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
495	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
496	                    baseline,
497	                    claim_records,
498	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
499	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
500	                    authority=plan.authority,
501	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
502	                    source_name=defn.name,
503	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
504	                )
505	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
506	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
507	                trail.record(
508	                    "score",
509	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
510	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
511	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
512	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
513	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
514	                )
515	                sc = scorecard
516	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
517	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
518	                track(
519	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
520	                )
521	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
522	                if ar is not None:
523	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
524	
525	        # ---- REPORT ----
526	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
527	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
528	            trail.record(
529	                "report",
530	                "assembled reproducibility package",
531	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
532	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
533	            )
534	
535	        if tuple(visited) != required:
536	            raise FirewallViolation(
537	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
538	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
539	            )
540	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
541	        ok = True
542	    except Exception as exc:
543	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
544	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
545	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
546	        try:
547	            trail.record(
548	                "run_failed",
549	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
550	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
551	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
552	                last_phase=last_phase,
553	            )
554	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
555	            pass
556	        raise
557	    finally:
558	        trace_ctx.close()
559	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
560	            try:
561	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
562	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
563	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
564	                pass
565	
566	    return StudyResult(
567	        study=defn.name,
568	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
569	        baseline=baseline,
570	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
571	        scorecard=scorecard,
572	        phases=tuple(visited),
573	        provenance=trail.entries,
574	        source_version=source_version,
575	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
576	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
577	    )
578

1	"""Deep-immutable, hashable mapping for the engine's frozen value records.
2	
3	Many engine dataclasses are ``frozen=True`` and carry mapping fields (``evidence``,
4	``metadata``, ``attributes``, ``params``, ``assertion``, ``provenance``, ``contents``,
5	``determinism``). With a plain ``dict`` default two problems arise (audit pass 1, issue #7):
6	
7	1. ``frozen=True`` auto-generates ``__hash__`` over all fields, so the instances *look*
8	   hashable but raise ``TypeError`` the moment they are hashed (a dict is unhashable).
9	2. ``frozen`` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays mutable in place,
10	   so a "frozen" provenance record can be silently altered after construction.
11	
12	:class:`FrozenDict` fixes both: it is an immutable :class:`~collections.abc.Mapping` and is
13	hashable. :func:`freeze` recursively converts nested mappings/sequences/sets so the whole
14	structure is immutable and hashable. Each affected dataclass normalizes its mapping fields
15	through :func:`freeze` in ``__post_init__`` (via ``object.__setattr__`` to satisfy frozen).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from collections.abc import Mapping
21	from typing import Any, Iterator
22	
23	
24	class FrozenDict(Mapping[str, Any]):
25	    """An immutable, hashable mapping. Item assignment is impossible; hashing is stable."""
26	
27	    __slots__ = ("_data", "_hash")
28	
29	    def __init__(self, data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
30	        # Freeze values so a directly-constructed FrozenDict satisfies its own
31	        # immutable+hashable contract even from nested-mutable input (audit pass 3, issue #27).
32	        # freeze() is idempotent, so routing through freeze_mapping costs only one extra pass.
33	        self._data: dict[str, Any] = (
34	            {k: freeze(v) for k, v in data.items()} if data is not None else {}
35	        )
36	        self._hash: int | None = None
37	
38	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
39	        return self._data[key]
40	
41	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[str]:
42	        return iter(self._data)
43	
44	    def __len__(self) -> int:
45	        return len(self._data)
46	
47	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48	        return f"FrozenDict({self._data!r})"
49	
50	    def __hash__(self) -> int:
51	        if self._hash is None:
52	            self._hash = hash(frozenset(self._data.items()))
53	        return self._hash
54	
55	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
56	        if isinstance(other, Mapping):
57	            return dict(self._data) == dict(other)
58	        return NotImplemented
59	
60	
61	def freeze(value: Any) -> Any:
62	    """Recursively convert ``value`` into an immutable, hashable form.
63	
64	    Mappings → :class:`FrozenDict`, lists/tuples → tuples, sets → frozensets. Known mutable
65	    buffers are converted to hashable equivalents (``bytearray`` → ``bytes``;
66	    ``array.array`` / numpy-like arrays exposing ``tobytes`` → a ``(kind, shape, bytes)``
67	    tuple). Any remaining leaf that is not hashable raises ``TypeError`` *here, at
68	    construction time* — rather than silently passing through and resurfacing the error the
69	    moment the record is hashed (audit pass 2, issue #19; the incomplete half of #7).
70	    """
71	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
72	        return value
73	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
74	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
75	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
76	        return tuple(freeze(v) for v in value)
77	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
78	        return frozenset(freeze(v) for v in value)
79	    if isinstance(value, bytearray):
80	        return bytes(value)
81	    # Duck-typed buffer/array support (e.g. array.array, numpy.ndarray) without importing
82	    # numpy: an array-like exposing tobytes() is canonicalized to a hashable descriptor tuple.
83	    # Require an array-ish marker (shape/typecode/dtype) so an unrelated object that merely
84	    # happens to have a tobytes() is left to the hashability check below (fix-review nit, #19).
85	    tobytes = getattr(value, "tobytes", None)
86	    array_like = hasattr(value, "shape") or hasattr(value, "typecode") or hasattr(value, "dtype")
87	    if callable(tobytes) and array_like and not isinstance(value, (bytes, str)):
88	        shape = getattr(value, "shape", None)
89	        kind = getattr(getattr(value, "dtype", None), "str", None) or getattr(
90	            value, "typecode", type(value).__name__
91	        )
92	        # Route the descriptor through freeze() so a malformed tobytes() returning a
93	        # non-hashable value is still caught loud at construction (audit pass 3, issue #26).
94	        return freeze((str(kind), tuple(shape) if shape is not None else None, tobytes()))
95	    try:
96	        hash(value)
97	    except TypeError as exc:
98	        raise TypeError(
99	            f"freeze: leaf of type {type(value).__name__!r} is not hashable and has no "
100	            "known immutable conversion; a frozen value record cannot contain it"
101	        ) from exc
102	    return value
103	
104	
105	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
106	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`."""
107	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
108

content.text: The fix is in place: `GateReview.__post_init__` calls `freeze_mapping(self.payload)`. Now let me find the regression test.

tests/test_pipeline.py:451:def test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable(tmp_path):
tests/test_pipeline.py:452:    # #113: GateReview's Mapping payload must be deep-frozen (immutable + hashable)
tests/test_pipeline.py:454:    from assay_engine.pipeline import GateReview
tests/test_pipeline.py:456:    r = GateReview(

451	def test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable(tmp_path):
452	    # #113: GateReview's Mapping payload must be deep-frozen (immutable + hashable)
453	    from assay_engine._frozen import FrozenDict
454	    from assay_engine.pipeline import GateReview
455	
456	    r = GateReview(
457	        gate="g",
458	        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
459	        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
460	        summary="s",
461	        payload={"a": {"b": 1}, "ids": [1, 2]},
462	    )
463	    assert isinstance(r.payload, FrozenDict)
464	    with pytest.raises(Exception):  # noqa: B017 — any mutation must fail
465	        r.payload["a"] = 2  # type: ignore[index]
466	    hash(r)  # frozen record is hashable
467	
468	
469	def test_run_study_correlates_spans_to_run_id(tmp_path):
470	    # #122: every phase span must see the run's assay.run_id baggage (traces correlate to the run)
471	    pytest.importorskip("opentelemetry")
472	    from contextlib import contextmanager
473	
474	    from opentelemetry import baggage
475	
476	    class CapturingTracer:
477	        def __init__(self):
478	            self.seen = set()
479	
480	        @contextmanager
481	        def span(self, name, attributes=None, *, kind="CHAIN"):
482	            self.seen.add(baggage.get_baggage("assay.run_id"))
483	            yield
484	
485	    class T:
486	        def start_run(self, name, params):
487	            return "run-xyz"
488	
489	        def log_metric(self, *a):
490	            pass
491	
492	        def log_artifact(self, *a):
493	            pass
494	
495	        def end_run(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
496	            pass
497	
498	    tracer = CapturingTracer()
499	    _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, tracer=tracer, tracker=T())
500	    assert tracer.seen == {"run-xyz"}  # all phase spans saw the run id; none None

content.text: The test covers all three claims: FrozenDict type, mutation raises, hashable. Let me run it and verify it actually catches a regression (would fail without the fix).
Now let me verify the test would actually fail without the fix (regression-catching power).

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
from dataclasses import dataclass, replace
from typing import Any, Mapping
from assay_engine.pipeline import GateReview, Phase
from assay_engine._frozen import FrozenDict

# Simulate the pre-fix behavior: a frozen dataclass WITHOUT the __post_init__ freeze
@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class GateReviewNoFix:
    gate: str
    frm: Phase
    to: Phase
    summary: str
    payload: Mapping[str, Any]

r = GateReviewNoFix(gate='g', frm=Phase.PREREGISTER, to=Phase.CONFIRM, summary='s', payload={'a': {'b':1}, 'ids':[1,2]})
print('isinstance FrozenDict:', isinstance(r.payload, FrozenDict))
try:
    r.payload['a'] = 2
    print('mutation succeeded (BUG present)')
except Exception as e:
    print('mutation raised:', type(e).__name__)
try:
    hash(r)
    print('hash ok')
except Exception as e:
    print('hash raised:', type(e).__name__)
"

content.text: Confirmed: without the fix, the test fails on all three assertions (not FrozenDict, mutation succeeds, hash raises TypeError). The test has genuine regression-catching power. Let me also confirm the fix produces deep-freezing (nested mapping under "a" is also frozen).

input.command:

from assay_engine._frozen import FrozenDict
r = GateReview(gate='g', frm=Phase.PREREGISTER, to=Phase.CONFIRM, summary='s', payload={'a': {'b':1}, 'ids':[1,2]})
print('payload type:', type(r.payload).__name__)
print('nested type:', type(r.payload['a']).__name__)  # deep-freeze
print('list->tuple:', type(r.payload['ids']).__name__, r.payload['ids'])

content.text:
The fix is sound and consistent with the codebase pattern. `GateReview.__post_init__` uses `freeze_mapping` exactly as `ProvenanceEntry` and the other frozen value records do. The payload is deep-frozen (nested mapping is FrozenDict, list becomes tuple), immutable, and the record is hashable.

input.reasoning: Issue #113: GateReview was a frozen dataclass with a mutable-in-place, unhashable Mapping payload (an escape of the codebase's own documented issue #7). The fix adds GateReview.__post_init__ (src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:90-92) which calls object.__setattr__('payload', freeze_mapping(self.payload)). This is the SAME pattern every other frozen mapping-bearing record uses (verified ProvenanceEntry in provenance.py:65-66 calls freeze_mapping identically), so it is the canonical root-cause fix, not a hack or over-claim. I confirmed at runtime that the fix deep-freezes: payload becomes a FrozenDict, nested mappings become FrozenDict, lists become tuples, and the record is hashable. The regression test test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable (tests/test_pipeline.py:451-466) asserts all three halves of the finding: isinstance FrozenDict, mutation raises, hash(r) succeeds. It passes on the current branch. Critically, I proved it has real regression-catching power by reconstructing the pre-fix dataclass (no __post_init__): payload was NOT a FrozenDict, in-place mutation succeeded (bug present), and hash() raised TypeError — i.e. the test would fail on every assertion without the fix. Both appropriate and complete are satisfied.
input.evidence: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:80-92 (GateReview frozen dataclass + __post_init__ freeze_mapping); src/assay_engine/_frozen.py:61-107 (freeze/freeze_mapping deep-immutable conversion); src/assay_engine/provenance.py:65-66 (ProvenanceEntry uses identical pattern — consistency); tests/test_pipeline.py:451-466 (regression test, passes). Pre-fix simulation: isinstance FrozenDict=False, mutation succeeded, hash raised TypeError — test catches the regression. Post-fix runtime: payload type=FrozenDict, nested type=FrozenDict, ids=tuple, hash ok. pytest: 1 passed.
